brent.label: Brent Crude $/bbl
brent.change: +58.0% since Feb 27 — Fri close $112.19; Sunday no trading; Investing.com open $106.41; Goldman warns could exceed $147.50
brent.tooltip: Brent Crude: The international benchmark. Pre-war close: $71.00/bbl (Feb 27). Mar 20 (Fri) close: $112.19/bbl (+3.26% on day). Sunday Mar 22: no US trading. Investing.com open $106.41, suggesting softening on GL U waiver expectations. Hit $119.11 intraday Mar 19. Goldman Sachs warned Brent 'likely to exceed 2008 all-time high ($147.50)' if disruptions persist. IEA: 'largest supply disruption in history.' Source: ICE Futures Europe / CNBC / Fortune / Investing.com.

wti.label: WTI Crude $/bbl
wti.change: +47.8% since Feb 27 — Fri close $98.32; Sunday no trading; gasoline futures at 3.5-year high
wti.tooltip: West Texas Intermediate: The US benchmark. Pre-war close: $66.50/bbl (Feb 27). Mar 20 (Fri) close: $98.32/bbl (+2.27%). Sunday Mar 22: no US trading. Investing.com open $95.00. RBOB gasoline futures +5.09% Fri, 3.5-year high. IEA: at least 8 mbd curtailed. Goldman worst case: additional $42/bbl through 2027. Source: NYMEX / CNBC / Barchart / Investing.com.

gas.label: US Gas Avg/Gal
gas.change: +$0.95 / +31.9% since Feb 27 — highest since Oct 2022 — CA >$5.00 — diesel $5.07
gas.tooltip: National average retail gasoline price. Pre-war: $2.98/gal (Feb 27). AAA Mar 21: $3.93/gal (+31.9% since war began). Highest since October 2022. California >$5.00/gal. Diesel $5.07 (highest since 2022). Georgia suspended fuel taxes. Jones Act waived Mar 18. Source: AAA via NBC News / EIA.

sp500.label: S&P 500
sp500.change: -373 pts / -5.4% — new 2026 low; 3 consecutive weekly losses; Sunday no trading
sp500.tooltip: S&P 500 index. Pre-war close: 6,878.88 (Feb 27). Mar 20 (Fri) close: 6,506.48 (−1.51%) — new 2026 low, below 200-day MA for 2nd day. 3 consecutive weekly losses. RSI 23.34 (deep oversold). Only 43.9% of stocks above 200-day MAs. VIX 26.78. Dow 45,577.47 (−0.96%). Nasdaq 21,647.61 (−2.01%). Sunday: no trading. Goldman year-end target 7,600 (adverse: 6,300). JPMorgan cut to 7,200. Source: NYSE / CNBC / Goldman / JPMorgan.

daily_cost.label: Est. Daily US Cost
daily_cost.tooltip: Estimated daily operational cost. Day 23: CENTCOM Adm. Cooper video update — 8,000+ targets struck, 8,000+ combat sorties, 130 naval vessels destroyed. GBU-72 5,000-lb bunker-busters destroying underground coastal missile facilities. 50,000+ US troops in CENTCOM AOR. CSIS/WSJ/The Hill estimate $1.0-1.43B/day. $200B supplemental pending White House→Congress submission. Source: CENTCOM / CSIS / WSJ.

total_cost.label: Est. Total Cost (23 Days)
total_cost.note: Pentagon $11.3B Day 6 + CSIS $16.5B Day 12 + ~$1B/day ongoing — ~$27-33B range — $200B supplemental pending
total_cost.tooltip: Cumulative estimated cost since Feb 28. Pentagon: $11.3B through Day 6. CSIS: $16.5B through Day 12. Extrapolated through Day 23 at ~$1-1.43B/day: ~$27-33B range. No official Day 23 total released. Penn Wharton: $40-95B direct + up to $210B total economic impact. $200B supplemental pending (faces Republican resistance). FY2027 War Dept budget: $1.5T (record). Source: Pentagon / CSIS / Penn Wharton / The Intercept.

targets_struck.label: Targets Struck
targets_struck.note: CENTCOM: 8,000+ US — 8,000+ sorties — 130 vessels destroyed — ~24,000+ combined est.
targets_struck.tooltip: Total targets struck by US and Israeli forces since Feb 28. CENTCOM Adm. Cooper (Mar 22 video): 8,000+ US targets, 8,000+ combat sorties flown, 130 Iranian naval vessels destroyed. GBU-72 bunker-busters on underground coastal missile facility. Iran's combat capability 'on steady decline.' Iranian missile/drone rate down 90%. Combined US/Israeli extrapolated ~24,000+. Source: CENTCOM / Adm. Brad Cooper Mar 22 / IDF.

us_kia.label: US Military Deaths
us_kia.note: 13 KIA confirmed — no new deaths through Day 23
us_kia.tooltip: US military deaths. 7 by enemy fire: 6 at Port Shuaiba, Kuwait (Day 2), 1 died of wounds at Prince Sultan AB (Day 9). 6 killed in KC-135 crash over Iraq (Day 13). No new US deaths reported through Day 23. Source: CENTCOM / TIME (Mar 17).

us_wia.label: US Service Members WIA
us_wia.note: ~200 total — vast majority minor — 180+ returned to duty
us_wia.tooltip: US service members wounded/injured across all theaters since Feb 28. ~200 total, vast majority minor. 180+ returned to duty. 50,000+ US service members deployed to CENTCOM AOR. Source: DoD / CENTCOM.

iranian_killed.label: Iranian Killed (Hengaw est.)
iranian_killed.note: Hengaw: 5,300 (511 civ) — HRANA: 3,114 — Health Min: 1,444 — attack rate down 90%
iranian_killed.tooltip: Iranian casualties — significant discrepancies across sources. Hengaw Organization (Mar 17): 5,300 killed (511 civilian, 4,789 military). HRANA (Mar 17): 3,114 deaths (1,354 civ, 1,138 mil, 622 uncl.). Iran Health Ministry via Al Jazeera: 1,444 killed, 18,551 injured. CNN composite: ~3,000+ across region. 3.7x divergence between Health Ministry and Hengaw. Iranian missile/drone attack rates down 90%. Source: Hengaw / HRANA / Iran Health Ministry / Al Jazeera / CNN.

lebanese_killed.label: Lebanese Killed
lebanese_killed.note: 1,001+ killed incl. 67 women, 111 children — 1M+ displaced (19% of pop.) — IDF 'wave of extensive strikes' Day 23
lebanese_killed.tooltip: Lebanese casualties from Israeli strikes and IDF ground operations since March 2. 1,001+ killed including 67 women and 111 children (~Mar 20). 1M+ displaced (~19% of population). IDF announced new 'wave of extensive strikes' on Hezbollah in southern Lebanon and Dahiyeh (Beirut) on Day 23. 570+ Hezbollah operatives killed. 280+ Hezbollah attacks claimed. ~100 rockets/day into Israel. Source: Lebanon Health Ministry / OCHA / IDF.

displaced.label: Displaced Persons
displaced.note: Iran: 3.2M + Lebanon: 1M+ — 24,600+ Afghans returned — 3,000+ vessels stranded — UNHCR / OCHA
displaced.tooltip: People displaced from their homes across all fronts. Iran: up to 3.2M people (UNHCR Mar 12). Lebanon: ~1M (~19% of population, OCHA ~Mar 20). 125,000+ Lebanese crossed into Syria. 24,600+ Afghans returned to Afghanistan (UNHCR Mar 22). 3,000+ vessels stranded in ME (IMO Mar 21). 40,000+ sailors stranded. Internet at ~4% of normal. Source: UNHCR / OCHA / IMO / NetBlocks.

flights_cancelled.label: Flights Cancelled
flights_cancelled.note: 52,000+ flights — 3,000+ vessels stranded — 40,000+ sailors — 21+ vessel attacks — 7+ seafarer deaths
flights_cancelled.tooltip: Commercial flights cancelled due to airspace closures: 52,000+ since Feb 28 (Travel Tourister/Cirium). 3,000+ vessels stranded in Middle East (IMO via NPR Mar 21). 40,000+ seafarers stranded. ~150 ships anchored outside Hormuz, ~170 containerships (450,000 TEU) trapped inside Gulf. 15,000+ cruise passengers on 6+ ships. 21+ maritime attacks, 7+ seafarer fatalities. JMIC/UKMTO: CRITICAL. Insurance at 5% of vessel value. Source: IMO / IATA / Bloomberg / UKMTO.

children_killed.label: Children Killed
children_killed.note: UNICEF: 296+ (Iran 200+, Lebanon 111+, Israel 4, Kuwait 1) — 1,800+ killed or injured
children_killed.tooltip: Children killed in the conflict. UNICEF (Mar 12): 296+ children killed — Iran 200+ (incl. ~168 at Minab school Day 1), Lebanon 91+, Israel 4, Kuwait 1. UNICEF (post-Mar 12): 1,800+ children killed or injured across region. 'One classroom of children every day' — UNICEF Deputy Chief Ted Chaiban Mar 22. Dimona/Arad strikes Day 22: 12-year-old boy and 5-year-old girl hospitalized in serious condition. Source: UNICEF / Iranian Red Crescent / NYT / Amnesty / Lebanese MoPH.
